title: thelypteris pilosohispida
intro: Thelypteris pilosohispida, also known as the hairy spleenwort, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The hairy spleenwort can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues.
